metaTitle: Export Table to LaTeX Online — Free | {{BRAND_NAME}}
metaDescription: Export any table to clean LaTeX code in one click. Get publication-ready LaTeX output for academic papers and journals. Free, no signup.
heroHeadline: Build your table visually. Get clean LaTeX output.
heroSubtext: No more writing LaTeX table syntax by hand. Build your table in Tablesmit, click Export, and get correctly formatted LaTeX code — ready to paste into your paper or thesis.

benefits.heading: Correct LaTeX syntax — every time
benefits.body: The export generates valid tabular environments with proper column alignment, horizontal rules, and escaped special characters. No manual debugging of mismatched braces or broken ampersands.

benefits.heading: Built for academic publishing
benefits.body: LaTeX is the standard for mathematics, physics, economics, and computer science journals. Tablesmit's LaTeX export is designed for researchers who need their tables in publication-ready format without learning table syntax.

benefits.heading: Column alignment preserved
benefits.body: Text columns export as left-aligned (l). Number and Currency columns export as right-aligned (r). Percentage and centred content uses (c). The alignment follows your column types automatically.

benefits.heading: Special characters escaped
benefits.body: Ampersands, underscores, percent signs, dollar signs, and other LaTeX-reserved characters are escaped automatically. Paste the output directly — no manual find-and-replace.

steps.heading: Build your table
steps.body: Set up your columns, add headers, fill in your data. Set column types — Text, Number, Currency — so the LaTeX alignment is generated correctly.

steps.heading: Click LaTeX in the Export panel
steps.body: Find the Export section in the right sidebar. Click LaTeX. The export generates immediately.

steps.heading: Paste into your document
steps.body: Copy the generated LaTeX code and paste it into your .tex file. The table renders correctly in any LaTeX distribution — pdfLaTeX, XeLaTeX, LuaLaTeX.

outputExample.description: A four-column research data table with a header row exports as:

useCases: Exporting a data table from a research study into a journal submission formatted in LaTeX
Creating a methods comparison table for a thesis or dissertation without writing tabular syntax manually
Building a complex results table visually and exporting it for use in an Overleaf project
Converting an Excel or CSV dataset into a properly formatted LaTeX table for publication

_codex_notes.note: outputExample is an additional field for this page. Render as a code block between the steps and use cases sections — dark background (bg-[#1e293b]), monospace font, syntax highlighting if available. The heading and description appear above the code block. This gives academics a preview of exactly what they will get before they try the tool.
